title: Mildly Roasted Portuguese Tomato Paella
description: A hearty, grassy, and velvety Portuguese dish featuring roasted tomatoes, perfect for a hangover cure, made in a traditional paella pan. This kosher-friendly recipe is sure to delight.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the smoked paprika, garlic, and onion to the paella pan and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the calasparra rice to the paella pan and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the crushed tomatoes, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper to the paella pan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then transfer the paella pan to the preheated oven.
Roast the paella in the oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the paella pan from the oven and sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top.
Let the paella rest for 5 minutes before serving.
